revision.comment: 那閉神社（焼津市）の記事を新規作成
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 那閉神社 |画像= [[File:Nahe-jinja.jpg|thumb|center|]] |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[焼津市]]浜当目3-14-13 |位置 = |祭神 = [[事代主命]]
[[大国主命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 継体天皇3年 |本殿 = 流造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第3日曜日 |神事 = }} '''那閉神社'''（なへじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[焼津市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == * [[事代主命]] * [[大国主命]] == 概要 == [[File:Nahe-jinja-torii.jpg|thumb|]] [[神奈備山]]である当目山（[[虚空蔵山]]）の山裾に鎮座しているため、「虚空蔵さん」とも呼ばれる。 [[継体天皇]]3年、[[物部氏]]の勧請により創祀されたとの社伝がある。 はじめ鍋崎海中の神の岩に祀られたが、後に当目山（[[虚空蔵山]]）に遷されたともいう{{Cite web |author = 延喜式神社の調査 |url = http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/suruga/bun/sr130103-01.html |title = 那閉神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載の[[式内社]]。『[[駿河国神名帳]]』には、正五位下「奈閉天神」との記載がある。 [[江戸]]期には鍋嶋大明神、鍋神社、鍋崎大明神とも称した。 [[明治]]期の[[神仏分離]]に際して願成寺と分離した{{Cite web |author = 玄松子の記憶 |url = http://www.genbu.net/data/suruga/nahe_title.htm |title = 那閉神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 文化財 == '''焼津市指定有形文化財''' * 常夜灯：文政5年（1822年） == 摂末社 == 境内には次の3社が鎮座する。 '''境内社''' * 津島神社 - 祭神：[[須佐之男命]] * 青木神社 - 祭神：須藤左門 * 稲荷神社 - 祭神：豊受神、御食神、宇迦之御靈神 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:なへしんしや}} [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 那閉神社 |画像= [[File:Nahe-jinja.jpg|thumb|center|]] |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[焼津市]]浜当目3-14-13 |位置 = |祭神 = [[事代主命]]
[[大国主命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 継体天皇3年 |本殿 = 流造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第3日曜日 |神事 = }} '''那閉神社'''（なへじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[焼津市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == * [[事代主命]] * [[大国主命]] == 概要 == [[File:Nahe-jinja-torii.jpg|thumb|那閉神社の鳥居]] [[神奈備山]]である当目山（[[虚空蔵山]]）の山裾に鎮座しているため、「虚空蔵さん」とも呼ばれる。 [[継体天皇]]3年、[[物部氏]]の勧請により創祀されたとの社伝がある。 はじめ鍋崎海中の神の岩に祀られたが、後に当目山（[[虚空蔵山]]）に遷されたともいう{{Cite web |author = 延喜式神社の調査 |url = http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/suruga/bun/sr130103-01.html |title = 那閉神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載の[[式内社]]。『[[駿河国神名帳]]』には、正五位下「奈閉天神」との記載がある。 [[江戸]]期には鍋嶋大明神、鍋神社、鍋崎大明神とも称した。 [[明治]]期の[[神仏分離]]に際して願成寺と分離した{{Cite web |author = 玄松子の記憶 |url = http://www.genbu.net/data/suruga/nahe_title.htm |title = 那閉神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 文化財 == '''焼津市指定有形文化財''' * 常夜灯：文政5年（1822年） == 摂末社 == 境内には次の3社が鎮座する。 '''境内社''' * 津島神社 - 祭神：[[須佐之男命]] * 青木神社 - 祭神：須藤左門 * 稲荷神社 - 祭神：豊受神、御食神、宇迦之御靈神 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:なへしんしや}} [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]]

]] [[神体山|神奈備山]]である当目山（[[虚空蔵山 (静岡県)|虚空蔵山]]）の山裾に鎮座しているため、「虚空蔵さん」とも呼ばれる。 [[継体天皇]]3年（[[509年]]）、[[物部氏]]の勧請により創祀されたとの社伝がある。 はじめ鍋崎海中の神の岩に祀られたが、後に当目山（虚空蔵山）に遷されたともいう{{Cite web |author = 延喜式神社の調査 |url = http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/suruga/bun/sr130103-01.html |title = 那閉神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載の[[式内社]]。『[[駿河国神名帳]]』には、正五位下「奈閉天神」との記載がある。 [[江戸]]期には鍋嶋大明神、鍋神社、鍋崎大明神とも称した。 [[明治]]期の[[神仏分離]]に際して願成寺と分離した{{Cite web |author = 玄松子の記憶 |url = http://www.genbu.net/data/suruga/nahe_title.htm |title = 那閉神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 文化財 == '''焼津市指定有形文化財''' * 常夜灯：文政5年（1822年） == 摂末社 == 境内には次の3社が鎮座する。 '''境内社''' * 津島神社 - 祭神：[[須佐之男命]] * 青木神社 - 祭神：須藤左門 * 稲荷神社 - 祭神：[[トヨウケビメ|豊受神]]、御食神、[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御靈神]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}

]] [[神体山|神奈備山]]である当目山（[[虚空蔵山 (静岡県)|虚空蔵山]]）の山裾に鎮座しているため、「虚空蔵さん」とも呼ばれる。 [[継体天皇]]3年（[[509年]]）、[[物部氏]]の勧請により創祀されたとの社伝がある。 はじめ鍋崎海中の神の岩に祀られたが、後に当目山（虚空蔵山）に遷されたともいう{{Cite web|和書 |author = 延喜式神社の調査 |url = https://web.archive.org/web/20160122025445/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/suruga/bun/sr130103-01.html |title = 那閉神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載の[[式内社]]。『[[駿河国神名帳]]』には、正五位下「奈閉天神」との記載がある。 [[江戸]]期には鍋嶋大明神、鍋神社、鍋崎大明神とも称した。 [[明治]]期の[[神仏分離]]に際して願成寺と分離した{{Cite web |author = 玄松子の記憶 |url = http://www.genbu.net/data/suruga/nahe_title.htm |title = 那閉神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 祭神 == * [[事代主|事代主命]] * [[大国主|大国主命]] == 文化財 == '''焼津市指定有形文化財''' * 常夜灯：文政5年（1822年） == 摂末社 == 境内には次の3社が鎮座する。 '''境内社''' * 津島神社 - 祭神：[[須佐之男命]] * 青木神社 - 祭神：須藤左門 * 稲荷神社 - 祭神：[[トヨウケビメ|豊受神]]、御食神、[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御靈神]] == ギャラリー == Marunitsurukashiwa.png|まるにつるかしわ
[[三つ柏|丸に蔓柏]]（神紋） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== 外部リンク == *[https://nahe-shrine.jimdofree.com/ 那閉神社サイト] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なへしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:焼津市の歴史]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:焼津市の建築物]]

那閉神社の鳥居
]] [[神体山|神奈備山]]である当目山（[[虚空蔵山 (静岡県)|虚空蔵山]]）の山裾に鎮座しているため、「虚空蔵さん」とも呼ばれる。 [[継体天皇]]3年（[[509年]]）、[[物部氏]]の勧請により創祀されたとの社伝がある。 はじめ鍋崎海中の神の岩に祀られたが、後に当目山（虚空蔵山）に遷されたともいう{{Cite web|和書 |author = 延喜式神社の調査 |url = https://web.archive.org/web/20160122025445/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/suruga/bun/sr130103-01.html |title = 那閉神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載の[[式内社]]。『[[駿河国神名帳]]』には、正五位下「奈閉天神」との記載がある。 [[江戸]]期には鍋嶋大明神、鍋神社、鍋崎大明神とも称した。 [[明治]]期の[[神仏分離]]に際して願成寺と分離した{{Cite web |author = 玄松子の記憶 |url = http://www.genbu.net/data/suruga/nahe_title.htm |title = 那閉神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 祭神 == * [[事代主|事代主命]] * [[大国主|大国主命]] == 文化財 == '''焼津市指定有形文化財''' * 常夜灯：文政5年（1822年） == 摂末社 == 境内には次の3社が鎮座する。 '''境内社''' * 津島神社 - 祭神：[[須佐之男命]] * 青木神社 - 祭神：須藤左門 * 稲荷神社 - 祭神：[[トヨウケビメ|豊受神]]、御食神、[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御靈神]] == ギャラリー == Marunitsurukashiwa.png|まるにつるかしわ

{{nihongo|'''Nahe Shrine'''|那閉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Suruga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nahe Shrine'''|那閉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Suruga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nahe Shrine'''|那閉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Suruga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nahe Shrine'''|那閉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Suruga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nahe Shrine'''|那閉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Suruga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nahe Shrine'''|那閉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Suruga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nahe Shrine'''|那閉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Suruga Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{Infobox religious building | name = Nahe Shrine | native_name = 那閉神社 | image = Nahe-jinja.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Nahe Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.880478|138.336014}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|fa|کوتوشیرونوشی|fr|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主|ko|고토시로누시|ru|Котосиронуси|simple|Kotoshironushi|th|โคโตชิโรนูชิ|zh|事代主神|WD=Q8009611}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nahe Shrine'''|那閉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Suruga Province]].

revision.comment: 15 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 那閉神社 |画像 = [[File:Nahe-jinja.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|52|49.9|N|138|20|9.4|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[焼津市]]浜当目3-14-13 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 52 |緯度秒 = 49.9 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 = 9.4 |祭神 = [[事代主|事代主命]]
[[大国主|大国主命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
[[近代社格制度#諸社|旧郷社]] |創建 = [[継体天皇]]3年（[[509年]]） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第3日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''那閉神社'''（なへじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[焼津市]]浜当目に鎮座する[[神社]]。 == 概要 == [[File:Nahe-jinja-torii.jpg|thumb|
那閉神社の鳥居
]] [[神体山|神奈備山]]である当目山（[[虚空蔵山 (静岡県)|虚空蔵山]]）の山裾に鎮座しているため、「虚空蔵さん」とも呼ばれる。 [[継体天皇]]3年（[[509年]]）、[[物部氏]]の勧請により創祀されたとの社伝がある。 はじめ鍋崎海中の神の岩に祀られたが、後に当目山（虚空蔵山）に遷されたともいう{{Cite web|和書 |author = 延喜式神社の調査 |url = https://web.archive.org/web/20160122025445/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/suruga/bun/sr130103-01.html |title = 那閉神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載の[[式内社]]。『[[駿河国神名帳]]』には、正五位下「奈閉天神」との記載がある。 [[江戸]]期には鍋嶋大明神、鍋神社、鍋崎大明神とも称した。 [[明治]]期の[[神仏分離]]に際して願成寺と分離した{{Cite web |author = 玄松子の記憶 |url = http://www.genbu.net/data/suruga/nahe_title.htm |title = 那閉神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 祭神 == * [[事代主|事代主命]] * [[大国主|大国主命]] == 文化財 == '''焼津市指定有形文化財''' * 常夜灯：文政5年（1822年） == 摂末社 == 境内には次の3社が鎮座する。 '''境内社''' * 津島神社 - 祭神：[[須佐之男命]] * 青木神社 - 祭神：須藤左門 * 稲荷神社 - 祭神：[[トヨウケビメ|豊受神]]、御食神、[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御靈神]] == ギャラリー == Marunitsurukashiwa.png|まるにつるかしわ
[[三つ柏|丸に蔓柏]]（神紋） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== 外部リンク == *[https://nahe-shrine.jimdofree.com/ 那閉神社サイト] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なへしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:焼津市の歴史]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:焼津市の建築物]]

[[近代社格制度#諸社|旧郷社]] |創建 = [[継体天皇]]3年（[[509年]]） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第3日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''那閉神社'''（なへじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[焼津市]]浜当目に鎮座する[[神社]]。 == 概要 == [[File:Nahe-jinja-torii.jpg|thumb|
那閉神社の鳥居
]] [[神体山|神奈備山]]である当目山（[[虚空蔵山 (静岡県)|虚空蔵山]]）の山裾に鎮座しているため、「虚空蔵さん」とも呼ばれる。 [[継体天皇]]3年（[[509年]]）、[[物部氏]]の勧請により創祀されたとの社伝がある。 はじめ鍋崎海中の神の岩に祀られたが、後に当目山（虚空蔵山）に遷されたともいう{{Cite web|和書 |author = 延喜式神社の調査 |url = https://web.archive.org/web/20160122025445/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/suruga/bun/sr130103-01.html |title = 那閉神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載の[[式内社]]。『[[駿河国神名帳]]』には、正五位下「奈閉天神」との記載がある。 [[江戸]]期には鍋嶋大明神、鍋神社、鍋崎大明神とも称した。 [[明治]]期の[[神仏分離]]に際して願成寺と分離した{{Cite web |author = 玄松子の記憶 |url = http://www.genbu.net/data/suruga/nahe_title.htm |title = 那閉神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 祭神 == * [[事代主|事代主命]] * [[大国主|大国主命]] == 文化財 == '''焼津市指定有形文化財''' * 常夜灯：文政5年（1822年） == 摂末社 == 境内には次の3社が鎮座する。 '''境内社''' * 津島神社 - 祭神：[[須佐之男命]] * 青木神社 - 祭神：須藤左門 * 稲荷神社 - 祭神：[[トヨウケビメ|豊受神]]、御食神、[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御靈神]] == ギャラリー == Marunitsurukashiwa.png|まるにつるかしわ
[[三つ柏|丸に蔓柏]]（神紋） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== 外部リンク == *[https://nahe-shrine.jimdofree.com/ 那閉神社サイト] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なへしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:焼津市の歴史]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:焼津市の建築物]] ==End Japanese==

]] [[神体山|神奈備山]]である当目山（[[虚空蔵山 (静岡県)|虚空蔵山]]）の山裾に鎮座しているため、「虚空蔵さん」とも呼ばれる。 [[継体天皇]]3年（[[509年]]）、[[物部氏]]の勧請により創祀されたとの社伝がある。 はじめ鍋崎海中の神の岩に祀られたが、後に当目山（虚空蔵山）に遷されたともいう{{Cite web|和書 |author = 延喜式神社の調査 |url = https://web.archive.org/web/20160122025445/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/suruga/bun/sr130103-01.html |title = 那閉神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載の[[式内社]]。『[[駿河国神名帳]]』には、正五位下「奈閉天神」との記載がある。 [[江戸]]期には鍋嶋大明神、鍋神社、鍋崎大明神とも称した。 [[明治]]期の[[神仏分離]]に際して願成寺と分離した{{Cite web |author = 玄松子の記憶 |url = http://www.genbu.net/data/suruga/nahe_title.htm |title = 那閉神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 祭神 == * [[事代主|事代主命]] * [[大国主|大国主命]] == 文化財 == '''焼津市指定有形文化財''' * 常夜灯：文政5年（1822年） == 摂末社 == 境内には次の3社が鎮座する。 '''境内社''' * 津島神社 - 祭神：[[須佐之男命]] * 青木神社 - 祭神：須藤左門 * 稲荷神社 - 祭神：[[トヨウケビメ|豊受神]]、御食神、[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御靈神]] == ギャラリー == Marunitsurukashiwa.png|まるにつるかしわ
[[三つ柏|丸に蔓柏]]（神紋） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== 外部リンク == *[https://nahe-shrine.jimdofree.com/ 那閉神社サイト] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:焼津市の歴史]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:焼津市の建築物]]

]] {{jalink|神体山|Q1595289|神奈備山}}である当目山（{{jalink|虚空蔵山 (静岡県)|Q64712681|虚空蔵山}}）の山裾に鎮座しているため、「虚空蔵さん」とも呼ばれる。 {{jalink|継体天皇|Q349277}}3年（{{jalink|509年|Q30977}}）、{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}の勧請により創祀されたとの社伝がある。 はじめ鍋崎海中の神の岩に祀られたが、後に当目山（虚空蔵山）に遷されたともいう{{Cite web|和書 |author = 延喜式神社の調査 |url = https://web.archive.org/web/20160122025445/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/suruga/bun/sr130103-01.html |title = 那閉神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に記載の{{jalink|式内社}}。『{{jalink|駿河国神名帳}}』には、正五位下「奈閉天神」との記載がある。 {{jalink|江戸|Q215646}}期には鍋嶋大明神、鍋神社、鍋崎大明神とも称した。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}期の{{jalink|神仏分離|Q1970535}}に際して願成寺と分離した{{Cite web |author = 玄松子の記憶 |url = http://www.genbu.net/data/suruga/nahe_title.htm |title = 那閉神社 |accessdate = 2016-07-31 }}。 == 祭神 == * {{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|事代主命}} * {{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主命}} == 文化財 == '''焼津市指定有形文化財''' * 常夜灯：文政5年（1822年） == 摂末社 == 境内には次の3社が鎮座する。 '''境内社''' * 津島神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|須佐之男命}} * 青木神社 - 祭神：須藤左門 * 稲荷神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|トヨウケビメ|Q2983724|豊受神}}、御食神、{{jalink|ウカノミタマ|Q3080728|宇迦之御靈神}} == ギャラリー == Marunitsurukashiwa.png|まるにつるかしわ
{{jalink|三つ柏|Q65247832|丸に蔓柏}}（神紋） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== 外部リンク == *[https://nahe-shrine.jimdofree.com/ 那閉神社サイト] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:焼津市の歴史]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:焼津市の建築物]]

鳥居と拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|52|49.9|N|138|20|9.4|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|焼津市|Q696783}}浜当目3-14-13 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 52 |緯度秒 = 49.9 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 = 9.4 |祭神 = {{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|事代主命}}
{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|旧郷社}} |創建 = {{jalink|継体天皇|Q349277}}3年（{{jalink|509年|Q30977}}） |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第3日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''那閉神社'''（なへじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|焼津市|Q696783}}浜当目に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 === 概要 === [[File:Nahe-jinja-torii.jpg|thumb|
那閉神社の鳥居
]] {{jalink|神体山|Q1595289|神奈備山}}である当目山（{{jalink|虚空蔵山 (静岡県)|Q64712681|虚空蔵山}}）の山裾に鎮座しているため、「虚空蔵さん」とも呼ばれる。 {{jalink|継体天皇|Q349277}}3年（{{jalink|509年|Q30977}}）、{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}の勧請により創祀されたとの社伝がある。 はじめ鍋崎海中の神の岩に祀られたが、後に当目山（虚空蔵山）に遷されたともいう。 『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に記載の{{jalink|式内社}}。『{{jalink|駿河国神名帳}}』には、正五位下「奈閉天神」との記載がある。 {{jalink|江戸|Q215646}}期には鍋嶋大明神、鍋神社、鍋崎大明神とも称した。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}期の{{jalink|神仏分離|Q1970535}}に際して願成寺と分離した。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|事代主命}} * {{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主命}} === 文化財 === '''焼津市指定有形文化財''' * 常夜灯：文政5年（1822年） === 摂末社 === 境内には次の3社が鎮座する。 '''境内社''' * 津島神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|須佐之男命}} * 青木神社 - 祭神：須藤左門 * 稲荷神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|トヨウケビメ|Q2983724|豊受神}}、御食神、{{jalink|ウカノミタマ|Q3080728|宇迦之御靈神}} === ギャラリー === Marunitsurukashiwa.png|まるにつるかしわ
{{jalink|三つ柏|Q65247832|丸に蔓柏}}（神紋） === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}}

{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|旧郷社}} |創建 = {{jalink|継体天皇|Q349277}}3年（{{jalink|509年|Q30977}}） |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第3日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''那閉神社'''（なへじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|焼津市|Q696783}}浜当目に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 === 概要 === [[File:Nahe-jinja-torii.jpg|thumb|
那閉神社の鳥居
]] {{jalink|神体山|Q1595289|神奈備山}}である当目山（{{jalink|虚空蔵山 (静岡県)|Q64712681|虚空蔵山}}）の山裾に鎮座しているため、「虚空蔵さん」とも呼ばれる。 {{jalink|継体天皇|Q349277}}3年（{{jalink|509年|Q30977}}）、{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}の勧請により創祀されたとの社伝がある。 はじめ鍋崎海中の神の岩に祀られたが、後に当目山（虚空蔵山）に遷されたともいう。 『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に記載の{{jalink|式内社}}。『{{jalink|駿河国神名帳}}』には、正五位下「奈閉天神」との記載がある。 {{jalink|江戸|Q215646}}期には鍋嶋大明神、鍋神社、鍋崎大明神とも称した。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}期の{{jalink|神仏分離|Q1970535}}に際して願成寺と分離した。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|事代主命}} * {{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主命}} === 文化財 === '''焼津市指定有形文化財''' * 常夜灯：文政5年（1822年） === 摂末社 === 境内には次の3社が鎮座する。 '''境内社''' * 津島神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|須佐之男命}} * 青木神社 - 祭神：須藤左門 * 稲荷神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|トヨウケビメ|Q2983724|豊受神}}、御食神、{{jalink|ウカノミタマ|Q3080728|宇迦之御靈神}} === ギャラリー === Marunitsurukashiwa.png|まるにつるかしわ

[[File:Nahe-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|52|49.9|N|138|20|9.4|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Yaizu|Q696783}}Hamatome 3-14-13 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 52 |緯度秒 = 49.9 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 = 9.4 |祭神 = {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system#Other shrines|Q712534|former gōsha}} |創建 = Year 3 of {{jalink|Emperor Keitai|Q349277}} ({{jalink|509|Q30977}}) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Nahe Shrine''' (なへじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Hamatome, {{jalink|Yaizu|Q696783}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. === Overview === [[File:Nahe-jinja-torii.jpg|thumb|
Torii of Nahe Shrine
]] Because it stands at the foot of Tōme-yama ({{jalink|Kokūzō-yama (Shizuoka Prefecture)|Q64712681|Kokūzō-yama}}), a {{jalink|shintai-zan|Q1595289|kannabi mountain}}, it is also called "Kokūzō-san." Shrine tradition says it was founded in Year 3 of {{jalink|Emperor Keitai|Q349277}} ({{jalink|509|Q30977}}) through a solicitation by the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}}. It is also said that it was first worshiped at a sacred rock in the sea off Nabesaki, and later moved to Tōme-yama (Kokūzō-yama). It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} listed in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'. In the '{{jalink|Suruga no Kuni Jinmyōchō}}', it appears as "Nahe Tenjin" with Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q215646}}, it was also called Nabeshima Daimyōjin, Nabe Shrine, and Nabesaki Daimyōjin. During the {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} period's {{jalink|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}, it was separated from Ganjō-ji. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === '''Yaizu City designated tangible cultural property''' * Jōyatō stone lantern: Bunsei 5 (1822) === Sessha and massha === The following three shrines stand in the precincts. '''Precinct shrines''' * Tsushima Shrine - Deity: {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Aoki Shrine - Deity: Sudō Samon * Inari Shrine - Deities: {{jalink|Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|Toyouke no Kami}}, Miketsu-no-kami, {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami}} === Gallery === Marunitsurukashiwa.png|maru ni tsuru kashiwa
{{jalink|Mitsu-gashiwa|Q65247832|Maru ni Tsuru-gashiwa}} (shrine crest) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}}

(P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県焼津市浜当目3-14-13', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:那閉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q28682737}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称 = Nahe Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nahe-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|52|49.9|N|138|20|9.4|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Yaizu|Q696783}}Hamatome 3-14-13 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 52 |緯度秒 = 49.9 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 = 9.4 |祭神 = {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system#Other shrines|Q712534|former gōsha}} |創建 = Year 3 of {{jalink|Emperor Keitai|Q349277}} ({{jalink|509|Q30977}}) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Nahe Shrine''' (なへじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Hamatome, {{jalink|Yaizu|Q696783}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. === Overview === [[File:Nahe-jinja-torii.jpg|thumb|
Torii of Nahe Shrine
]] Because it stands at the foot of Tōme-yama ({{jalink|Kokūzō-yama (Shizuoka Prefecture)|Q64712681|Kokūzō-yama}}), a {{jalink|shintai-zan|Q1595289|kannabi mountain}}, it is also called "Kokūzō-san." Shrine tradition says it was founded in Year 3 of {{jalink|Emperor Keitai|Q349277}} ({{jalink|509|Q30977}}) through a solicitation by the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}}. It is also said that it was first worshiped at a sacred rock in the sea off Nabesaki, and later moved to Tōme-yama (Kokūzō-yama). It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} listed in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'. In the '{{jalink|Suruga no Kuni Jinmyōchō}}', it appears as "Nahe Tenjin" with Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q215646}}, it was also called Nabeshima Daimyōjin, Nabe Shrine, and Nabesaki Daimyōjin. During the {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} period's {{jalink|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}, it was separated from Ganjō-ji. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === '''Yaizu City designated tangible cultural property''' * Jōyatō stone lantern: Bunsei 5 (1822) === Sessha and massha === The following three shrines stand in the precincts. '''Precinct shrines''' * Tsushima Shrine - Deity: {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Aoki Shrine - Deity: Sudō Samon * Inari Shrine - Deities: {{jalink|Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|Toyouke no Kami}}, Miketsu-no-kami, {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami}} === Gallery === Marunitsurukashiwa.png|maru ni tsuru kashiwa
{{jalink|Mitsu-gashiwa|Q65247832|Maru ni Tsuru-gashiwa}} (shrine crest) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}}
=== External links === *[https://nahe-shrine.jimdofree.com/ Nahe Shrine website] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nahe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nahe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nahe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:焼津市の歴史]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:焼津市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: used local AI agents to translate these pages. Contrary to the description it was actually Claude Code for a few, OpenClaw with Codex, and regular Codex. Ollama and DeepSeek were too bad to actually use. see categorization since some of them are very questionable, especially if there was a massive decrease in page size or the {{jawiki| template is minimal or absent on the page

[[File:Nahe-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|52|49.9|N|138|20|9.4|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Yaizu|Q696783}}Hamatome 3-14-13 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 52 |緯度秒 = 49.9 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 = 9.4 |祭神 = {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system#Other shrines|Q712534|former gōsha}} |創建 = Year 3 of {{jalink|Emperor Keitai|Q349277}} ({{jalink|509|Q30977}}) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Nahe Shrine''' (なへじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Hamatome, {{jalink|Yaizu|Q696783}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. === Overview === [[File:Nahe-jinja-torii.jpg|thumb|
Torii of Nahe Shrine
]] Because it stands at the foot of Tōme-yama ({{jalink|Kokūzō-yama (Shizuoka Prefecture)|Q64712681|Kokūzō-yama}}), a {{jalink|shintai-zan|Q1595289|kannabi mountain}}, it is also called "Kokūzō-san." Shrine tradition says it was founded in Year 3 of {{jalink|Emperor Keitai|Q349277}} ({{jalink|509|Q30977}}) through a solicitation by the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}}. It is also said that it was first worshiped at a sacred rock in the sea off Nabesaki, and later moved to Tōme-yama (Kokūzō-yama). It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} listed in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'. In the '{{jalink|Suruga no Kuni Jinmyōchō}}', it appears as "Nahe Tenjin" with Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q215646}}, it was also called Nabeshima Daimyōjin, Nabe Shrine, and Nabesaki Daimyōjin. During the {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} period's {{jalink|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}, it was separated from Ganjō-ji. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === '''Yaizu City designated tangible cultural property''' * Jōyatō stone lantern: Bunsei 5 (1822) === Sessha and massha === The following three shrines stand in the precincts. '''Precinct shrines''' * Tsushima Shrine - Deity: {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Aoki Shrine - Deity: Sudō Samon * Inari Shrine - Deities: {{jalink|Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|Toyouke no Kami}}, Miketsu-no-kami, {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami}} === Gallery === Marunitsurukashiwa.png|maru ni tsuru kashiwa
{{jalink|Mitsu-gashiwa|Q65247832|Maru ni Tsuru-gashiwa}} (shrine crest) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}}
=== External links === *[https://nahe-shrine.jimdofree.com/ Nahe Shrine website] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nahe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nahe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nahe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:焼津市の歴史]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:焼津市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: used local AI agents to translate these pages. Contrary to the description it was actually Claude Code for a few, OpenClaw with Codex, and regular Codex. Ollama and DeepSeek were too bad to actually use. see categorization since some of them are very questionable, especially if there was a massive decrease in page size or the {{jawiki| template is minimal or absent on the page

{{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system#Other shrines|Q712534|former gōsha}} |創建 = Year 3 of {{jalink|Emperor Keitai|Q349277}} ({{jalink|509|Q30977}}) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Nahe Shrine''' (なへじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Hamatome, {{jalink|Yaizu|Q696783}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. === Overview === [[File:Nahe-jinja-torii.jpg|thumb|
Torii of Nahe Shrine
]] Because it stands at the foot of Tōme-yama ({{jalink|Kokūzō-yama (Shizuoka Prefecture)|Q64712681|Kokūzō-yama}}), a {{jalink|shintai-zan|Q1595289|kannabi mountain}}, it is also called "Kokūzō-san." Shrine tradition says it was founded in Year 3 of {{jalink|Emperor Keitai|Q349277}} ({{jalink|509|Q30977}}) through a solicitation by the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}}. It is also said that it was first worshiped at a sacred rock in the sea off Nabesaki, and later moved to Tōme-yama (Kokūzō-yama). It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} listed in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'. In the '{{jalink|Suruga no Kuni Jinmyōchō}}', it appears as "Nahe Tenjin" with Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q215646}}, it was also called Nabeshima Daimyōjin, Nabe Shrine, and Nabesaki Daimyōjin. During the {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} period's {{jalink|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}, it was separated from Ganjō-ji. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === '''Yaizu City designated tangible cultural property''' * Jōyatō stone lantern: Bunsei 5 (1822) === Sessha and massha === The following three shrines stand in the precincts. '''Precinct shrines''' * Tsushima Shrine - Deity: {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Aoki Shrine - Deity: Sudō Samon * Inari Shrine - Deities: {{jalink|Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|Toyouke no Kami}}, Miketsu-no-kami, {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami}} === Gallery === Marunitsurukashiwa.png|maru ni tsuru kashiwa
{{jalink|Mitsu-gashiwa|Q65247832|Maru ni Tsuru-gashiwa}} (shrine crest) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}}

(P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県焼津市浜当目3-14-13', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:那閉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q28682737}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称 = Nahe Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nahe-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|52|49.9|N|138|20|9.4|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Yaizu|Q696783}}Hamatome 3-14-13 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 52 |緯度秒 = 49.9 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 = 9.4 |祭神 = {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system#Other shrines|Q712534|former gōsha}} |創建 = Year 3 of {{jalink|Emperor Keitai|Q349277}} ({{jalink|509|Q30977}}) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Nahe Shrine''' (なへじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Hamatome, {{jalink|Yaizu|Q696783}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. === Overview === [[File:Nahe-jinja-torii.jpg|thumb|
Torii of Nahe Shrine
]] Because it stands at the foot of Tōme-yama ({{jalink|Kokūzō-yama (Shizuoka Prefecture)|Q64712681|Kokūzō-yama}}), a {{jalink|shintai-zan|Q1595289|kannabi mountain}}, it is also called "Kokūzō-san." Shrine tradition says it was founded in Year 3 of {{jalink|Emperor Keitai|Q349277}} ({{jalink|509|Q30977}}) through a solicitation by the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}}. It is also said that it was first worshiped at a sacred rock in the sea off Nabesaki, and later moved to Tōme-yama (Kokūzō-yama). It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} listed in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'. In the '{{jalink|Suruga no Kuni Jinmyōchō}}', it appears as "Nahe Tenjin" with Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q215646}}, it was also called Nabeshima Daimyōjin, Nabe Shrine, and Nabesaki Daimyōjin. During the {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} period's {{jalink|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}, it was separated from Ganjō-ji. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === '''Yaizu City designated tangible cultural property''' * Jōyatō stone lantern: Bunsei 5 (1822) === Sessha and massha === The following three shrines stand in the precincts. '''Precinct shrines''' * Tsushima Shrine - Deity: {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Aoki Shrine - Deity: Sudō Samon * Inari Shrine - Deities: {{jalink|Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|Toyouke no Kami}}, Miketsu-no-kami, {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami}} === Gallery === Marunitsurukashiwa.png|maru ni tsuru kashiwa
{{jalink|Mitsu-gashiwa|Q65247832|Maru ni Tsuru-gashiwa}} (shrine crest) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}}
=== External links === *[https://nahe-shrine.jimdofree.com/ Nahe Shrine website] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nahe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nahe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nahe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:焼津市の歴史]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:焼津市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[File:Nahe-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|52|49.9|N|138|20|9.4|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Yaizu|Q696783|WD=Q696783}}Hamatome 3-14-13 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 52 |緯度秒 = 49.9 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 = 9.4 |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#Other shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former gōsha}} |創建 = Year 3 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277}} ({{ill|lt=509|Q30977|WD=Q30977}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Nahe Shrine''' (なへじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Hamatome, {{ill|lt=Yaizu|Q696783|WD=Q696783}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. === Overview === [[File:Nahe-jinja-torii.jpg|thumb|
Torii of Nahe Shrine
]] Because it stands at the foot of Tōme-yama ({{ill|lt=Kokūzō-yama (Shizuoka Prefecture)|Q64712681|WD=Q64712681|lt=Kokūzō-yama}}), a {{ill|lt=shintai-zan|Q1595289|WD=Q1595289|lt=kannabi mountain}}, it is also called "Kokūzō-san." Shrine tradition says it was founded in Year 3 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277}} ({{ill|lt=509|Q30977|WD=Q30977}}) through a solicitation by the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}}. It is also said that it was first worshiped at a sacred rock in the sea off Nabesaki, and later moved to Tōme-yama (Kokūzō-yama). It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Suruga no Kuni Jinmyōchō|UNKNOWN}}', it appears as "Nahe Tenjin" with Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q215646|WD=Q215646}}, it was also called Nabeshima Daimyōjin, Nabe Shrine, and Nabesaki Daimyōjin. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} period's {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}, it was separated from Ganjō-ji. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === '''Yaizu City designated tangible cultural property''' * Jōyatō stone lantern: Bunsei 5 (1822) === Sessha and massha === The following three shrines stand in the precincts. '''Precinct shrines''' * Tsushima Shrine - Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} * Aoki Shrine - Deity: Sudō Samon * Inari Shrine - Deities: {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Kami}}, Miketsu-no-kami, {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami}} === Gallery === Marunitsurukashiwa.png|maru ni tsuru kashiwa
{{ill|lt=Mitsu-gashiwa|Q65247832|WD=Q65247832|lt=Maru ni Tsuru-gashiwa}} (shrine crest) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}}

[[File:Nahe-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|52|49.9|N|138|20|9.4|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Yaizu|Q696783|WD=Q696783}}Hamatome 3-14-13 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 52 |緯度秒 = 49.9 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 = 9.4 |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#Other shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former gōsha}} |創建 = Year 3 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277}} ({{ill|lt=509|Q30977|WD=Q30977}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Nahe Shrine''' (なへじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Hamatome, {{ill|lt=Yaizu|Q696783|WD=Q696783}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. === Overview === [[File:Nahe-jinja-torii.jpg|thumb|
Torii of Nahe Shrine
]] Because it stands at the foot of Tōme-yama ({{ill|lt=Kokūzō-yama (Shizuoka Prefecture)|Q64712681|WD=Q64712681|lt=Kokūzō-yama}}), a {{ill|lt=shintai-zan|Q1595289|WD=Q1595289|lt=kannabi mountain}}, it is also called "Kokūzō-san." Shrine tradition says it was founded in Year 3 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277}} ({{ill|lt=509|Q30977|WD=Q30977}}) through a solicitation by the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}}. It is also said that it was first worshiped at a sacred rock in the sea off Nabesaki, and later moved to Tōme-yama (Kokūzō-yama). It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Suruga no Kuni Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}}', it appears as "Nahe Tenjin" with Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q215646|WD=Q215646}}, it was also called Nabeshima Daimyōjin, Nabe Shrine, and Nabesaki Daimyōjin. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} period's {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}, it was separated from Ganjō-ji. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === '''Yaizu City designated tangible cultural property''' * Jōyatō stone lantern: Bunsei 5 (1822) === Sessha and massha === The following three shrines stand in the precincts. '''Precinct shrines''' * Tsushima Shrine - Deity: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}} * Aoki Shrine - Deity: Sudō Samon * Inari Shrine - Deities: {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Kami}}, Miketsu-no-kami, {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami}} === Gallery === Marunitsurukashiwa.png|maru ni tsuru kashiwa
{{ill|lt=Mitsu-gashiwa|Q65247832|WD=Q65247832|lt=Maru ni Tsuru-gashiwa}} (shrine crest) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}}

{{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#Other shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former gōsha}} |創建 = Year 3 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277}} ({{ill|lt=509|Q30977|WD=Q30977}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Nahe Shrine''' (なへじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Hamatome, {{ill|lt=Yaizu|Q696783|WD=Q696783}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. === Overview === [[File:Nahe-jinja-torii.jpg|thumb|
Torii of Nahe Shrine
]] Because it stands at the foot of Tōme-yama ({{ill|lt=Kokūzō-yama (Shizuoka Prefecture)|Q64712681|WD=Q64712681|lt=Kokūzō-yama}}), a {{ill|lt=shintai-zan|Q1595289|WD=Q1595289|lt=kannabi mountain}}, it is also called "Kokūzō-san." Shrine tradition says it was founded in Year 3 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277}} ({{ill|lt=509|Q30977|WD=Q30977}}) through a solicitation by the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}}. It is also said that it was first worshiped at a sacred rock in the sea off Nabesaki, and later moved to Tōme-yama (Kokūzō-yama). It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Suruga no Kuni Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}}', it appears as "Nahe Tenjin" with Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q215646|WD=Q215646}}, it was also called Nabeshima Daimyōjin, Nabe Shrine, and Nabesaki Daimyōjin. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} period's {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}, it was separated from Ganjō-ji. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties === '''Yaizu City designated tangible cultural property''' * Jōyatō stone lantern: Bunsei 5 (1822) === Sessha and massha === The following three shrines stand in the precincts. '''Precinct shrines''' * Tsushima Shrine - Deity: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}} * Aoki Shrine - Deity: Sudō Samon * Inari Shrine - Deities: {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Kami}}, Miketsu-no-kami, {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami}} === Gallery === Marunitsurukashiwa.png|maru ni tsuru kashiwa
{{ill|lt=Mitsu-gashiwa|Q65247832|WD=Q65247832|lt=Maru ni Tsuru-gashiwa}} (shrine crest) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}}

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Suruga no Kuni Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}}', it appears as "Nahe Tenjin" with Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q215646|WD=Q215646|1=Edo}}, it was also called Nabeshima Daimyōjin, Nabe Shrine, and Nabesaki Daimyōjin. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period's {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, it was separated from Ganjō-ji. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} === Cultural properties === '''Yaizu City designated tangible cultural property''' * Jōyatō stone lantern: Bunsei 5 (1822) === Sessha and massha === The following three shrines stand in the precincts. Shrine tradition says it was founded in Year 3 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277|1=Emperor Keitai}} ({{ill|lt=509|Q30977|WD=Q30977|1=509}}) through a solicitation by the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}. It is also said that it was first worshiped at a sacred rock in the sea off Nabesaki, and later moved to Tōme-yama (Kokūzō-yama). It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Suruga no Kuni Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}}', it appears as "Nahe Tenjin" with Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q215646|WD=Q215646|1=Edo}}, it was also called Nabeshima Daimyōjin, Nabe Shrine, and Nabesaki Daimyōjin. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period's {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, it was separated from Ganjō-ji. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} === Cultural properties === '''Yaizu City designated tangible cultural property''' * Jōyatō stone lantern: Bunsei 5 (1822) === Sessha and massha === The following three shrines stand in the precincts. ]] Because it stands at the foot of Tōme-yama ({{ill|lt=Kokūzō-yama (Shizuoka Prefecture)|Q64712681|WD=Q64712681|lt=Kokūzō-yama|1=Mount Kokuzo}}), a {{ill|lt=shintai-zan|Q1595289|WD=Q1595289|lt=kannabi mountain|1=Sacred mountains}}, it is also called "Kokūzō-san." Shrine tradition says it was founded in Year 3 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277|1=Emperor Keitai}} ({{ill|lt=509|Q30977|WD=Q30977|1=509}}) through a solicitation by the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}. It is also said that it was first worshiped at a sacred rock in the sea off Nabesaki, and later moved to Tōme-yama (Kokūzō-yama). It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Suruga no Kuni Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}}', it appears as "Nahe Tenjin" with Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q215646|WD=Q215646|1=Edo}}, it was also called Nabeshima Daimyōjin, Nabe Shrine, and Nabesaki Daimyōjin. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period's {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, it was separated from Ganjō-ji. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} === Cultural properties === '''Yaizu City designated tangible cultural property''' * Jōyatō stone lantern: Bunsei 5 (1822) === Sessha and massha === The following three shrines stand in the precincts. '''Precinct shrines''' * Tsushima Shrine - Deity: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Aoki Shrine - Deity: Sudō Samon * Inari Shrine - Deities: {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}}, Miketsu-no-kami, {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} === Gallery === Marunitsurukashiwa.png|maru ni tsuru kashiwa

{{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#Other shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = Year 3 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277|1=Emperor Keitai}} ({{ill|lt=509|Q30977|WD=Q30977|1=509}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Nahe Shrine''' (なへじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hamatome, {{ill|lt=Yaizu|Q696783|WD=Q696783|1=Yaizu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. === Overview === [[File:Nahe-jinja-torii.jpg|thumb|
Torii of Nahe Shrine
]] Because it stands at the foot of Tōme-yama ({{ill|lt=Kokūzō-yama (Shizuoka Prefecture)|Q64712681|WD=Q64712681|lt=Kokūzō-yama|1=Mount Kokuzo}}), a {{ill|lt=shintai-zan|Q1595289|WD=Q1595289|lt=kannabi mountain|1=Sacred mountains}}, it is also called "Kokūzō-san." Shrine tradition says it was founded in Year 3 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277|1=Emperor Keitai}} ({{ill|lt=509|Q30977|WD=Q30977|1=509}}) through a solicitation by the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}. It is also said that it was first worshiped at a sacred rock in the sea off Nabesaki, and later moved to Tōme-yama (Kokūzō-yama). It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Suruga no Kuni Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}}', it appears as "Nahe Tenjin" with Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q215646|WD=Q215646|1=Edo}}, it was also called Nabeshima Daimyōjin, Nabe Shrine, and Nabesaki Daimyōjin. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period's {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, it was separated from Ganjō-ji. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} === Cultural properties === '''Yaizu City designated tangible cultural property''' * Jōyatō stone lantern: Bunsei 5 (1822) === Sessha and massha === The following three shrines stand in the precincts. '''Precinct shrines''' * Tsushima Shrine - Deity: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Aoki Shrine - Deity: Sudō Samon * Inari Shrine - Deities: {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}}, Miketsu-no-kami, {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} === Gallery === Marunitsurukashiwa.png|maru ni tsuru kashiwa
{{ill|lt=Mitsu-gashiwa|Q65247832|WD=Q65247832|lt=Maru ni Tsuru-gashiwa|1=Maru ni Tsuru-gashiwa}} (shrine crest) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|52|49.9|N|138|20|9.4|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yaizu|Q696783|WD=Q696783|1=Yaizu}}Hamatome 3-14-13 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 52 |緯度秒 = 49.9 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 = 9.4 |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#Other shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = Year 3 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277|1=Emperor Keitai}} ({{ill|lt=509|Q30977|WD=Q30977|1=509}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Nahe Shrine''' (なへじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hamatome, {{ill|lt=Yaizu|Q696783|WD=Q696783|1=Yaizu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. === Overview === [[File:Nahe-jinja-torii.jpg|thumb|
Torii of Nahe Shrine
]] Because it stands at the foot of Tōme-yama ({{ill|lt=Kokūzō-yama (Shizuoka Prefecture)|Q64712681|WD=Q64712681|lt=Kokūzō-yama|1=Mount Kokuzo}}), a {{ill|lt=shintai-zan|Q1595289|WD=Q1595289|lt=kannabi mountain|1=Sacred mountains}}, it is also called "Kokūzō-san." Shrine tradition says it was founded in Year 3 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277|1=Emperor Keitai}} ({{ill|lt=509|Q30977|WD=Q30977|1=509}}) through a solicitation by the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}. It is also said that it was first worshiped at a sacred rock in the sea off Nabesaki, and later moved to Tōme-yama (Kokūzō-yama). It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Suruga no Kuni Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}}', it appears as "Nahe Tenjin" with Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q215646|WD=Q215646|1=Edo}}, it was also called Nabeshima Daimyōjin, Nabe Shrine, and Nabesaki Daimyōjin. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period's {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, it was separated from Ganjō-ji. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} === Cultural properties === '''Yaizu City designated tangible cultural property''' * Jōyatō stone lantern: Bunsei 5 (1822) === Sessha and massha === The following three shrines stand in the precincts. '''Precinct shrines''' * Tsushima Shrine - Deity: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Aoki Shrine - Deity: Sudō Samon * Inari Shrine - Deities: {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}}, Miketsu-no-kami, {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} === Gallery === Marunitsurukashiwa.png|maru ni tsuru kashiwa
{{ill|lt=Mitsu-gashiwa|Q65247832|WD=Q65247832|lt=Maru ni Tsuru-gashiwa|1=Maru ni Tsuru-gashiwa}} (shrine crest) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}}

{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#Other shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = Year 3 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|qid=Q349277|1=Emperor Keitai}} ({{ill|lt=509|Q30977|qid=Q30977|1=509}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''Nahe Shrine''' (なへじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hamatome, {{ill|lt=Yaizu|Q696783|qid=Q696783|1=Yaizu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. === Overview === [[File:Nahe-jinja-torii.jpg|thumb|
Torii of Nahe Shrine
]] Because it stands at the foot of Tōme-yama ({{ill|lt=Kokūzō-yama (Shizuoka Prefecture)|Q64712681|qid=Q64712681|lt=Kokūzō-yama|1=Mount Kokuzo}}), a {{ill|lt=shintai-zan|Q1595289|qid=Q1595289|lt=kannabi mountain|1=Sacred mountains}}, it is also called "Kokūzō-san." Shrine tradition says it was founded in Year 3 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|qid=Q349277|1=Emperor Keitai}} ({{ill|lt=509|Q30977|qid=Q30977|1=509}}) through a solicitation by the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|qid=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}. It is also said that it was first worshiped at a sacred rock in the sea off Nabesaki, and later moved to Tōme-yama (Kokūzō-yama). It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Suruga no Kuni Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}}', it appears as "Nahe Tenjin" with Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q215646|qid=Q215646|1=Edo}}, it was also called Nabeshima Daimyōjin, Nabe Shrine, and Nabesaki Daimyōjin. During the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period's {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|qid=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, it was separated from Ganjō-ji. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} === Cultural properties === '''Yaizu City designated tangible cultural property''' * Jōyatō stone lantern: Bunsei 5 (1822) === Sessha and massha === The following three shrines stand in the precincts. '''Precinct shrines''' * Tsushima Shrine - Deity: {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Aoki Shrine - Deity: Sudō Samon * Inari Shrine - Deities: {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|qid=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}}, Miketsu-no-kami, {{ill|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} === Gallery === Marunitsurukashiwa.png|maru ni tsuru kashiwa
